data.title: Image Resources Sets AGM Date as It Advances Growth and Value-Add Strategy
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Image Resources NL ( (AU:IMA) ) has shared an announcement.

Image Resources NL has scheduled its annual general meeting for 28 May 2026, with the re-election of directors on the agenda and a 9 April 2026 deadline for receipt of director nominations at its registered office. The meeting comes as the company continues successful operations at its Atlas project and advances its broader strategy to expand production, diversify products and enhance value through a lower-emissions, provisionally patented process to upgrade ilmenite to synthetic rutile, reinforcing its position in the global critical minerals market.

Image’s transition from a single-mine, single-product supplier into a multi-mine, multi-product producer selling into global markets underscores its ambition to grow scale and sustainability in mineral sands. The company’s focus on innovation, including demonstrating the technical and economic feasibility of its synthetic rutile process in 2026, has potential implications for operating efficiency, environmental footprint and long-term competitiveness for investors and customers alike.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:IMA) stock is a Hold with a A$0.05 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Image Resources NL stock, see the AU:IMA Stock Forecast page.

More about Image Resources NL

Image Resources NL is an Australian mineral sands miner focused on supplying critical minerals such as titanium dioxide, zircon and monazite containing rare earth elements to global markets. The company has a track record of on-time, on-budget project delivery, including the Boonanarring mine and the Atlas project in Western Australia, and is pursuing a growth strategy involving multiple mines, diversified products and broader international customer reach.

Average Trading Volume: 450,695

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: A$44.28M

data.title: Taiyo Holdings Backs KKR-Led Tender Offer to Go Private and Accelerate Growth
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from Taiyo Holdings Co., Ltd. ( (JP:4626) ).

Taiyo Holdings has agreed that KJ005 Co., Ltd., owned by investment funds managed by KKR, will launch a tender offer to take the company private, a move its board supports as of March 31, 2026. Management expects that private ownership under KKR will secure a more stable shareholder base, enable faster, long-term oriented decision-making, and leverage KKR’s global network and sector expertise to accelerate growth and structural reforms across its semiconductor and medical-related businesses.

Under KKR’s ownership, Taiyo plans to continue executing its long-term management plan “Beyond Imagination 2030” while pursuing additional “Beyond Boundaries” initiatives that it believes would be difficult to realize alone. The company views the transaction as expanding growth opportunities and enhancing medium- to long-term corporate value, asserting that it aligns with the common interests of shareholders by supporting sustained strategic execution rather than short-term market pressures.

The most recent analyst rating on (JP:4626) stock is a Buy with a Yen6005.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Taiyo Holdings Co., Ltd. stock, see the JP:4626 Stock Forecast page.

More about Taiyo Holdings Co., Ltd.

Taiyo Holdings Co., Ltd., listed on the Tokyo Stock Exchange Prime Market under code 4626, operates in businesses tied to the semiconductor and medical and pharmaceutical fields. Through its portfolio, the company focuses on growth in technology-driven markets, positioning itself to capitalize on rising demand in advanced electronics and healthcare-related sectors.

Average Trading Volume: 346,631

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: Yen575B

data.title: Regener8 Resources Sets May 2026 General Meeting, Moves to Digital Notices
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Regener8 Resources NL ( (AU:R8R) ) has issued an announcement.

Regener8 Resources NL has called a general meeting of shareholders for 6 May 2026 in West Perth, setting 4 May 2026 as the record date to determine voting eligibility. The company will hold the meeting in person, with all resolutions decided by poll, and is encouraging shareholders to vote in advance or appoint the chair as proxy.

In line with recent changes to the Corporations Act, Regener8 will not mail hard copies of the notice of meeting unless shareholders have specifically elected to receive them, instead providing electronic access via the ASX website. The notice also outlines proxy voting rules and stresses the importance of timely submission of proxy forms to ensure votes are counted, underscoring the company’s focus on regulatory compliance and shareholder participation.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:R8R) stock is a Hold with a A$0.19 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Regener8 Resources NL stock, see the AU:R8R Stock Forecast page.

More about Regener8 Resources NL

Regener8 Resources NL is an Australian-listed company in the resources sector, with a shareholder base governed under the Corporations Act 2001 (Cth). The company’s securities trade on the ASX, and it conducts its corporate governance and shareholder communications in line with Australian regulatory requirements for listed resource companies.

Clara Resources Australia has lodged an Appendix 3B outlining a proposed issue of up to 250 million new ordinary fully paid shares and 270 million unlisted options exercisable at $0.006 and expiring in March 2030. data.title: UraniumX Raises $1.05 Million in Flow-Through Share Financing for Athabasca Exploration
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An announcement from Stearman Resources, Inc. ( (TSE:STMN) ) is now available.

UraniumX Discovery Corp. has closed the first tranche of a non-brokered private placement, issuing 6,176,471 flow-through shares at $0.17 each for gross proceeds of just over $1.05 million. The securities are subject to a statutory hold period until August 1, 2026, and the company paid $73,500 in cash finders’ fees connected to the financing.

The proceeds will fund Canadian exploration expenses to be renounced to investors and will primarily support ongoing and planned exploration at the Murphy Lake and Zoo Bay uranium properties. The financing strengthens UraniumX’s ability to advance its Athabasca Basin exploration portfolio, underpinning its efforts to progress high-potential uranium targets in a globally significant mining jurisdiction.

More about Stearman Resources, Inc.

UraniumX Discovery Corp. is a Canadian uranium exploration company focused on advancing high-potential assets in Saskatchewan’s Athabasca Basin, one of the world’s leading uranium districts. Its portfolio includes the Murphy Lake, Zoo Bay, and NeoCore properties, located along the basin’s eastern margin near established infrastructure and known high-grade deposits, where it employs geophysics, drilling, and academic collaborations to enhance discovery potential.

Average Trading Volume: 333,233

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Macmahon Holdings Limited ( (AU:MAH) ) just unveiled an update.

Macmahon Holdings Limited has notified the market that 870,256 performance rights have lapsed, after conditions attached to those rights were not met or became incapable of being satisfied as of 27 February 2026. The cessation of these securities slightly reduces the company’s potential future equity dilution under its incentive arrangements, and signals that certain performance or service hurdles tied to these rights were not achieved, which may be relevant for investors tracking management incentives and capital structure.

The notification, lodged as an Appendix 3H with the ASX, confirms that the affected securities are performance rights rather than ordinary shares. While the announcement is administrative in nature, it provides transparency on issued capital and may influence how the market assesses Macmahon’s remuneration outcomes, alignment of incentives with performance, and any future need for updated equity-based incentive plans.

data.title: MedMira Expands McKesson Partnership as Quarterly Revenue Falls and Loss Widens
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Medmira ( (TSE:MIR) ) just unveiled an update.

MedMira reported second-quarter fiscal 2026 results marked by weaker revenue and a wider net loss, alongside a strategic expansion of its distribution network. The company signed a major agreement with McKesson in the U.S. and Canada, broadening access to private, state and federal customers for its FDA and Health Canada approved rapid tests and boosting brand visibility.

Operationally, MedMira advanced its pipeline with strong clinical data for the Multiplo Complete Syphilis test published in a peer-reviewed journal and steady progress toward Canadian approval of its TP/HIV self-test. Financially, revenue fell to $24,503 amid order delays linked to a U.S. government shutdown, while operating expenses were reduced, liabilities rose, working capital deficit widened slightly and debt restructuring talks continued, underscoring ongoing going-concern pressures.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:MIR) stock is a Sell with a C$0.06 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Medmira stock, see the TSE:MIR Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on MIR Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, MIR is a Neutral.

The score is primarily weighed down by weak financial performance (ongoing losses, cash burn, negative equity, and high leverage). Technical indicators add mild downside bias (negative MACD and price below the 200-day average), while valuation is constrained by loss-making status and no dividend support.

To see Spark’s full report on MIR stock, click here.

More about Medmira

MedMira Inc. is a Halifax-based medical diagnostics company that develops and owns Rapid Vertical Flow (RVF) technology for rapid testing. Its Reveal, Multiplo and Miriad branded assays provide instant diagnosis for infectious diseases such as HIV and hepatitis C, serving hospitals, laboratories, clinics and individual consumers in global markets.

data.title: Earth’s Energy downplays impact of geothermal JV shareholder dispute in ASX response
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from Cradle Resources Limited ( (AU:EE1) ).

Earth’s Energy Limited has responded to an ASX query regarding its December disclosure of a notice of dispute from minority shareholders in its geothermal joint venture. The company said it chose to inform the market for transparency, but did not classify the announcement as price sensitive because the board judged the claims to be weak and the JV assets to be of limited strategic significance.

The board emphasised that it does not expect the dispute to materially affect Earth’s Energy’s operations or the value of its securities, citing minimal expenditure on the JV assets and a broader strategic focus beyond the geothermal project. The company detailed that it received the dispute notice on 24 December 2025, assessed the allegations and formed its materiality view by 29 December, and then lodged its announcement before market open on 30 December in line with continuous disclosure obligations.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:EE1) stock is a Sell with a A$0.01 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Cradle Resources Limited stock, see the AU:EE1 Stock Forecast page.

More about Cradle Resources Limited

Earth’s Energy Limited is an ASX-listed company focused on geothermal energy assets held through a joint venture structure. The company has previously indicated that its broader strategy includes assessing additional resources projects and assets, suggesting that its long-term growth is not solely dependent on the existing geothermal joint venture portfolio.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: A$3.75M
